channel.title: PhotoShop on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in PhotoShop on yexca'Blog

item.title: Batch Process Images with Photoshop Automation Scripts
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash

This article was written by Hiyoung

Original article: https://blog.hiyoung.icu/2022/08/18/5be9517606f2/

Batch Process Images with Photoshop Automation Scripts

  1. Click Window -> Actions in the menu bar (Shortcut: Alt+F9).
  2. At the bottom of the tab, click ‘Create New Set’ (New Folder), name it ‘Resize Images’ (you can rename it yourself).
  3. Then click ‘Create New Action’ next to it. You’ll see the record button at the bottom turn red.
  4. Click File > Open, open one of the images you want to process, modify its size, then click OK. Then ‘Save As’, give it a name, and save. (Do NOT use ‘Export’, otherwise the action won’t record correctly, leading to an infinite loop.)
  5. Click File > Automate > Batch.
  6. Settings:
    1. Under ‘Play’, select the ‘Set’ and ‘Action’.
    2. Under ‘Source’, select the folder containing the images to modify.
    3. Under ‘Destination’, select the folder to save the modified images.
    4. Check ‘Override Action “Open” Commands’ and ‘Override Action “Save As” Commands’.

Content partially referenced from the internet.
